ops.body:
Discrepancy found while landing the hosted serve backend (phase 1): the deliverable's "GPU memory snapshots targeting ~70s cold starts" claim is not reproducible from the artifacts. The port (athome/serve_modal.py) configures no `enable_memory_snapshot=True` on `@app.function` and no `@modal.enter(snap=True)` — a regex sweep for `snapshot|enable_memory|snap=True` returns zero hits — and the spike it faithfully mirrors (.spike-reference/modal_app.py) is likewise snapshot-free. The ~70s figure, if real, was read off stdout during the interactive spike session and is not encoded anywhere.

Consequently the accepted readiness-budget fix (serve.py: ManagedServer.wait_healthy now derives its deadline from the backend, and ModalServeBackend.ready_timeout_s returns ModalVllmSettings.startup_timeout=900s) sizes the client-side wait for a full UN-snapshotted cold boot, not the phantom ~70s. No snapshot support was added unilaterally.

Owner decision requested: either (a) amend this task's deliverable / the decision record to drop the snapshot claim, or (b) schedule GPU memory-snapshot support as explicit new work in a later phase.
